idea: NorMuon-arxiv: row-wise neuron normalization + second-order momentum AFTER Muon orthogonalization (not stacked-tensor variant)
evidence: arxiv 2510.05491 'NorMuon: Making Muon more efficient and scalable' +11.31% training efficiency over Muon at 1.1B scale
notes: Different from normuon-04 (modded-nanogpt stacked-QKV variant). Maintains per-neuron 2nd-order stats and applies row normalization post-NS-orthogonalization. Adds second-moment buffer similar to Adam.

idea: Newton-Muon: add right preconditioning via input second moment to Muon (interpret Muon as implicit Newton method)
evidence: arxiv 2604.01472 Apr2026 'The Newton-Muon Optimizer' 6% fewer iterations and 4% wall-clock reduction on GPT-2 pretraining
notes: Very recent paper (Apr 2026). Standard Muon neglects right preconditioning from input second moment. Newton-Muon adds this. Risk: memory cost of storing input second moment matrix per layer.
